title: Mohanlal Babubhai Navdiwala vs Sushilaben Wd/O Manharbhai Prabhubhai ... on 10 June, 2025
body: Mohanlal Babubhai Navdiwala vs Sushilaben Wd/O Manharbhai Prabhubhai ... on 10 June, 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined IN THE HIGH COURT OF GUJARAT AT AHMEDABAD R/CIVIL REVISION APPLICATION NO. 323 of 2017 FOR APPROVAL AND SIGNATURE: HONOURABLE MR.JUSTICE SANJEEV J.THAKER ================================================================ Approved for Reporting Yes No Yes ================================================================ MOHANLAL BABUBHAI NAVDIWALA & ORS. Versus SUSHILABEN WD/O MANHARBHAI PRABHUBHAI PATEL & ORS. ================================================================ Appearance: MR SP MAJMUDAR(3456) for the Applicant(s) No. 1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8 MR NK MAJMUDAR(430) for the Opponent(s) No. 1,2,3,4 ================================================================ CORAM:HONOURABLE MR.JUSTICE SANJEEV J.THAKER Date : 10/06/2025 ORAL JUDGMENT 1. Rule. The present Civil Revision Application under Section 115 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 (For short 'The CPC ') has been filed challenging the order dated 13.04.2017 passed by 9th Additional Senior Civil Judge, Surat, below Exhibit-14, in Civil Suit No.227 of 2016, whereby the application under the provisions of Order vii Rule 11 has been rejected with the consent of the parties for final hearing. Page 1 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined 2. For the sake of brevity and convenience, the parties are referred to at their original status as that in Special Civil Suit No.227 of 2016. 3. The brief facts arising in the present Civil Revision Application are as under:- 3.1 The plaintiff filed Special Civil Suit No.227 of 2016, stating that the father of the plaintiff, Manharbhai P. Patel, has purchased the suit property by registered sale deed on 29.01.2005 and that as there was a mistake in the description of the property, a supplementary rectification deed was executed on 27.02.2007 and pursuant to the same, the name of the father of the plaintiff Manharbhai P. Patel was mutated by way of revenue entry No.3220, dated 07.08.2009. It is the case of the plaintiff in the plaint that the father of the plaintiff Manharbhai P. Patel had good relation with defendant - Mohanlal Babubhai Navdiwala and because of the said relation Manharbhai P. Patel has signed certain documents. It is the case of the plaintiff that Page 2 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined documents have been fabricated by the defendant No.1 with respect to the suit property. It has been stated in the plaint that defendant No.1 has concocted agreement executed on 26.12.2011 and the plaintiff came to know of all the facts of the execution of defendants by theirfather only when defendant No.1 applied before the concerned authority to convert the property to non agricultural land. It is the case of the plaintiff that in Special Civil Suit No.57 of 2012 by fraud and misrepresentation on the father of plaintiff, consent terms have been filed. It has been alleged in the plaint that by fraud, the sale deed has been executed in favour of the defendants which have been registered as registration No.784/2012. The plaintiff has stated that the defendant had informed their father Manharbhai P. Patel, that an amount of Rs.20/- lakh is to be shown as sale consideration in the sale deed, and thereafter, the said amount of Rs.20/- lakh will be returned by the plaintiff to the defendant and the defendant shall develop the said property and it is because of the trust that the father of the Page 3 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined plaintiff had on defendant No.1, the father of the plaintiff Manharbhai P. Patel executed the documents and it is only after the execution of sale deed No.784/2012, that the defendant did not take late Manharbhai P. Patel into confidence in any of the decision taken by the defendants and because of the said shock of fraud that has been committed on late Manharbhai P. Patel, he was hospitalized and eventually expired on 24.03.2013. 3.2 It is the case of the plaintiff in the plaint, that there was temporary entry No.3649 dated 20.4.2012, that when the notice under Section 135(d) with respect to the temporary entry No.3649, dated 20.04.2013, was received by the plaintiffs, the plaintiff came to know about execution of the sale deed, and thereafter, the plaintiffs filed RTS Case No.55 of 2013 and the cause of action to file the suit has arisen when the plaintiff received the notice under Section 135(d) , and therefore, it is the case of the plaintiff that the suit is within the period of limitation. The plaintiff in the Page 4 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined plaint has sought for a relief to declare the sale deed, registered No.784/2012, which is registered on 07.11.2012, to be illegal and that the same has been executed by fraud and a relief has been sought for injunction. The defendant appeared in the said suit and filed application Exhibit-14 under the provisions of Order vii Rule 11(d) of CPC and after hearing the parties, the said application has been rejected. Hence, the present Revision Application. Submissions of learned advocate for the defendant 4. Learned advocate for the petitioner-defendant has mainly submitted that the suit that has been filed by the plaintiff is hopelessly time barred, before the institution of the present suit the defendants had filed Civil Suit No.57 of 2012, against the father of the present plaintiffs and the reliefs were sought in the said suit were for specific performance of the agreement dated 07.02.2005 and possession receipt dated 26.12.2011 with respect to the suit property and in the said suit a compromise was arrived at Page 5 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined between the plaintiffs of the said suit i.e. the defendants herein and the father of the plaintiffs i.e. Manharbhai P. Patel, wherein it was agreed by the father of the plaintiffs herein, that all the documents executing have been admitted by the father of the plaintiff and wherein the father of the plaintiff i.e. Manharbhai P. Patel had agreed to execute the sale deed in favour of the defendants, and the said compromise was arrived and pursuant to the said compromise purshis a consent decree has been passed in Civil Suit No.57 of 2012 and the Special Civil Suit No.57 of 2012 has been disposed of. 5. It has also been argued that from the plain reading of the plaint, it can be established that the plaint is hopelessly time barred, as the plaintiff himself has stated that because of the alleged fraud committed by the defendant, the father of the plaintiff late Manharbhai P. Patel was in shock and has expired. Learned advocate for the defendant has argued that during the life time of late Manharbhai P. Patel, he has Page 6 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined neither challenged the said sale deed nor has challenged the execution of the document signed by late Manharbhai P. Patel, on the contrary, in Civil Suit No.57 of 2012, late Manharbhai P. Patel has compromised the said suit and has admitted all the documents, and therefore, the cause of action to file the suit arose for the first time in the year 2005 when the agreement to sale was executed on 07.05.2005 and the sale deed having been executed on 07.11.2012 and as per Article-58 of the Limitation Act, the suit for cancellation of the sale deed has to be executed within a period of 3 years, from the date of agreement to sell in the year 2005, and therefore, in the present case plaintiff has executed different documents with respect to the suit property in the year 2005, when the plaintiff alleges that a fraud was committed on late Manharbhai P. Patel. It has been argued by learned advocate for the plaintiff that the plaintiff has in the plaint stated that the cause of action to file the suit has arisen, when the plaintiff was issued the notice under Section 135(d) and admittedly the last notice that has been issuedis on Page 7 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined 23.04.2013. Learned advocate for the defendant has also argued that the last notice that was sent by the revenue authority under the provision of section 135(d) as per village form No.6 qua the last notice was served on 23.04.2013 and by way of additional affidavit the plaintiff has tried to canvass his argument that even if the version of the plaintiff is believed, the last notice that has been served with respect to the suit property is dated 23.04.2013, and therefore, it can be clearly established that the suit that has been filed by the plaintiff is hopelessly time barred and the trial Court could not have rejected the application under the provisions of Order vii Rule 11 of the CPC . 6. Learned advocate for the defendant has relied on the judgment reported in (1) 2024 (0) AIJEL-HC-249324 in the case of Dhaniben D/o Morarbhai Bapubhai Rohit and W/of Parshottambhai Chhotabhai Rohit v. Rohit Kanubhai Morarbhai, learned advocate for the defendant has also relied on the judgment reported in (2) 2019 (0) AIJEL-HC Page 8 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined 240810 in the case of Emrald Co Operative Housing Society Limited v. Gulamkadar S/o Gulam Husain Abdulkadar and Bai Shakarbu Decd., (3) 2019 SCC OnLine SC 372 in the case of Raghwendra Sharan Singh v. Ram Prasanna Singh (Dead) by Lrs . and it is the case of the defendant that looking to the averments made in the plaint and the document annexed with the plaint the suit is hopelessly time barred required to be rejected as being barred by law. Submissions of learned advocate for the plaintiff 7. Learned advocate for the plaintiff has mainly argued that while deciding the application under Order vii Rule 11 of the CPC the Court cannot look at the defence put forward by the defendant in written statement nor can be the documents relied on by the defendant alongwith his written statement can be perused while deciding an application under Order vii Rule 11 of the CPC . 8. The learned advocate for the plaintiff has also argued that the plaintiff has categorically stated that the cause of Page 9 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined action to file the suit has arisen when the notice under Section 135(d) of the Land Revenue Code were served on the plaintiff, and therefore, the suit cannot be stated to be time barred. 9. Learned advocate for the plaintiff has also argued that the plaintiff has clearly stated in the plaint that a fraud has been committed on the plaintiff's father and the plaintiff's father executed different documents with respect to the suit property in the year 2005 and the plaintiff has also taken a contention that it was only because of good relation between the father of the plaintiff Manharbhai P. Patel and defendant No.1-Mohanbhai Babubhai Navdiwala that documents were signed by late Manharbhai P. Patel. Learned advocate for the plaintiff has also argued that there was no intention of the father of the plaintiff to execute the sale deed in favour of the defendants and there were also no money transactions, more particularly, the plaintiff has also denied the receipt of payment of Rs.20/- lakh and that the Page 10 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined said fraud came to the knowledge of plaintiff recently and the suit is filed within the period of limitation, and therefore, it has been argued that the suit cannot be said to be barred by law more particularly by law of limitation. Learned advocate for the plaintiff has relied on the judgment reported in (1) 2025 (0) AIJEL-HC 249968 in the case of Lh. of Patel Naranbhai Karmasibhai v. Lh. of Harijan Dhyabhai Bhudarbhai, (2) 2022 (0) AIJEL-HC-244690 in the case of Dilipkumar Jayantilal Shah v. Yasinbhai Faridbhai Hokabaj , (3) 2021 (0) AIJEL-HC 243278 in the case of Bardoli Shreerang Exhibitors Private Limited v. Maheshbhai Babubhai Hirpara , (4) 2024 (0) AIJEL-SC 74555 in the case of Daliben Valjibhai v. Prajapati KodarbhaiKachrabhai, (5) 2020 (0) AIJEL-SC 66299 in the case of Shakti Bhog Food Industries Ltd. v. Central Bank of India , (6) 2020 (0) AIJEL- HC 62061 in the case of Chhotaben v. Kiritbhaijalkrushnabhai Thakkar , therefore, it has been argued that after taking into consideration the plaint and the documents annexed with the plaint the trial Court has Page 11 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined rightly rejected the said application as the plaintiff will have to be led oral evidence to prove his case with respect to the fraud that has been committed by the defendants on the father of the plaintiff. Analysis 10. Having heard learned advocate for the parties and after considering the plaint and the documents annexed with the plaint, the following facts are admitted facts:- (i) It is the case of the defendant that (a) the plaintiff Manharbhai P. Patel executed different documents, more particularly agreement to sale on 07.02.2005 (b) possession receipt dated 26.11.2011. (ii) The defendant filed Civil Suit No.57 of 2012 against the father of the plaintiff. (iii) By compromise Exhibit-15, the said Civil Suit No.57 of 2012 was disposed of by a consent decree in which the father Page 12 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined of the plaintiff admitted execution of all the documents. (iv) The sale deed has been executed by the father of the plaintiff on 07.11.2012 wherein the father of the plaintiff admits of giving an application before the concerned department to convert the property into non agricultural land and the said sale deed also states about dispute between the father of the plaintiffs and the defendants and the same being resolved and on amount of Rs.20/- lakh having been received by the father of the plaintiff and pursuant thereto the sale deed has been executed by the father of the plaintiff. (v) In the plaint, the plaintiff has stated that the father of the plaintiff expired on 24.04.2013. (vi) In the plaint, it has been mentioned that the father of the plaintiff expired because of the shock that the father of the plaintiff got because of the acts done by the defendants. (vii) The plaintiffs has in the plaint stated that the cause of action to file the suit has arisen when the plaintiff received Page 13 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined notice under section 135(d) of the Land Revenue Code. (viii) The suit that has been filed by the plaintiff on 11.05.2016 and from the documentary evidence the last notice that has been served by the Revenue Authority is dated 23.04.2013. 11. In view of the above referred facts, the fact remains that though the father of the plaintiff was alive till 24.04.2013, the father of the plaintiff has not challenged the sale deed that has been executed by him on 07.11.2012. The fact also remains that from the averments made in the plaint it can be seen that the plaintiff's father was aware of the execution of all the documents that have been executed by him during his life time, in Civil Suit No.57 of 2012, which is for specific performance of the agreement to sale with respect to the suit property, in the suit also the father of the plaintiff could have raised the issue that all the said documents have been signed by him by force and that he does not intend to sell the suit property to the defendants. Page 14 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined But on the contrary, the father of the plaintiff has admitted the said documents and has also agreed to execute the sale deed in favour of the defendants. 12. The sale deed that has also been executed by the father of the plaintiff states about filing of an application before the concerned department to get the land converted into non agricultural land. The said sale deed also talks about the dispute that were there between the father of the plaintiff and the defendants and that the same has been resolved by the parties, and therefore, the sale deed has been executed. It is not the case of the plaintiff that the father of the plaintiff was not aware of execution of the said sale deed. At Paragraph No.3 of the plaint, the plaintiff states that because of the acts done by the defendants, the father of the plaintiff was in a shock and because of the same, he has expired on 24.04.2013. The fact also remains that the plaintiff in the plaint has not given any explanation as to why the father of the plaintiff during his life time have not Page 15 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined challenged the said sale deed executed on 07.11.2012 and the only fact that has been stated in the plaint is that the cause of action to file the suit has arisen only after receipt of notice under section 135(d) of the Land Revenue Code. The fact that the plaintiff is stating at paragraph No.3 of the plaint that the father of the plaintiff had died because of illegal act done by the defendant on the father of the plaintiff clearly states that the plaintiffs were aware during the life time of the father of the plaintiff that the sale deed has been executed and by making vague allegations and pleadings have tried to bring the suit within the period of limitation. 13. Remedy under order VII Rule 11 of CPC are independent and special remedy, wherein the Court is empowered to summarily dismiss the suit at threshold. The Court has to be satisfied that the action should be terminated at the state on any of the grounds contained under the said provision. Page 16 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined 14. If by full reading of the plaint if the Court finds that the suit is vexatious and does not find any merit the Court would be justified in exercising of the powers under Order VII Rule 11 of the Code of Civil Procedure and if the Court finds that the suit is barred by law the Court shall have no option but to reject the plaint. 15. In the present case, the plaintiff has deliberately not mentioned the date of receipt of the notice under Section 135(d) of Land Revenue Code and the said fact of receiving of notice is mentioned and referred to in the plaint. Moreover, the said notice is referred to in the plaint, and therefore, the said document i.e. notice is incorporated by a reference in the plaint will have to be considered. In the present case, late Manharbhai P. Patel died in the year 2013 and during the life time of late Manharbhai P. Patel the said sale deed has remain unchallenged and the same has been challenged by the son and wife of late Manharbhai P. Patel, after the period of limitation. The fact that the father of the Page 17 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined plaintiff was aware of the alleged fraud being committed on him and the fact that the plaintiffs have stated that the father of the plaintiff died because of the said act done by the defendant clearly states that even before the death of father of the plaintiff, the plaintiffs were aware of execution of the sale deed and the said sale deed having not been challenged by the plaintiff within the period of limitation, the suit is hopelessly time barred. 16. In the case of Dhaniben D/o Morarbhai Bapubhai Rohit and W/of Parshottambhai Chhotabhai Rohit v. Rohit Kanubhai Morarbhai, wherein it has been held at paragraph No.6.3 are as under:- "6.3 Reliance placed on an order of the Hon'ble Supreme Court in the case of Keshav Sood (supra) wherein in Para 5 the Supreme Court has observed that while rejecting a plaint the Court cannot go into the documents produced by the defendants. The observations were made by the Hon'ble Supreme Court in context of a plea by the defendant-applicant of Order VII Rule XI on the issue of res-judicata by referring to certain documents. Here, in the facts of the present case when the Trial Court found that the foundational facts themselves were not correctly stated, the Court Page 18 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined when found that it was a case of suppression of facts the Court had to consider the documents of the defendant on the issue of suppression made by the plaintiff of the very documents which should have been in his custody. The contention of the Counsel for the appellants cannot be accepted. When the case of the plaintiff was based on a false claim on suppression of facts which were material to the issue, the Trial Court committed no error in relying on the documents produced by the defendants. The documents produced by the defendants indicated that notices under Section 135D were issued and acknowledgements were produced. This belied a statement in the plaint that no notices under Section 135-D were received. The case was further exposed to suppression when signed release deeds were placed on record which struck at the plaintiffs' root to further their case for partition of properties. Only when these documents were produced did the plaintiffs come forward then to change track to suggest that the signatures on the release deed was forged. 17. In the present case, it has admitted that the plaintiff has received a notice under Section 135(d) of the Land Revenue Code and if the said fact is to be pleaded as per the document, last notice that was served by the Land Revenue Department is of 23.04.2013 and the fact of the said case will be applicable to the present facts of the case. 18. In the case of Emrald Co Operative Housing Society Page 19 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined Limited v. Gulamkadar S/o Gulam Husain Abdulkadar and Bai Shakarbu Decd., wherein it has been held at paragraph No.31 are as under :- "31. So far as the cause of action issue which has been agitated has generated a debate amongst the learned Senior Advocates that such point has not been agitated before the court below and as such not available to the petitioner in the present proceedings. In this context, if the main object of Order 7 Rule 11 is to be looked into, this special power has been assigned to the court to curb the abuse of the process and ill-motive by launching litigation and to see that untenable proceedings may not waste public time and money. The very object of provision related to rejection of plaint is to curb such kind of litigation and it has been held by catena of decisions that irrespective of the mode of litigation, it is the duty of the Court itself to examine the tenability of such kind of litigation and as such, this hyper-technical plea which has been taken by learned Senior Advocate appearing for respondents that such point cannot be examined by the Court appears to be devoid of merit and non-dealing of such contention would rather defeat the very object of Order 7 Rule 11(d) of CPC . So, irrespective of objection, if the cause of action which has been stipulated in the plaint is examined as a part of smart pleading by concealing material facts, a bald assertion is made as if the plaintiffs came to know only in the year 2010, precisely on 24.2.2010, but the said bald assertion appears to be completely misleading since it is based upon suppression of material facts. Furthermore, the cause of action is to be ascertained from the surrounding circumstances and if the afore- mentioned circumstances in sequence are to be examined, the cause of action, which has been mentioned in paragraph No.4 of the plaint would clearly indicate that it is a part and parcel of a smart pleading and nothing else to bring the suit within the period of limitation. Such attempt in the considered opinion of the Page 20 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined Court is not to be encouraged particularly in view of this peculiar set of circumstances. If all these issues analytically are to be examined, it would lead to a situation that impugned order which has been passed is absolutely laconic, based upon general principle just to evade exercise of jurisdiction which is vested in law and reflects a clear non-application of mind. The aforesaid circumstances, which are apparent on the record encircled with the averments of the plaint and the documents attached to it need not create a situation where evidence at length is to be led. It may be that limitation is a mixed question of law and fact but on the basis of aforesaid undisputed facts, which are reflecting on record, this general principle in the peculiar background of fact is not possible to be digested just to allow to go on with a speculative litigation which has been generated after loosing from revenue proceedings. The Court, therefore, is of the opinion that order impugned in the revision application is laconic, based upon ignorance of material reflecting on record and also based upon non-application of mind. No valid and effective reasons are reflecting to justify the conclusion in dismissing the application which has been filed by the petitioner and as such, the combined reading of aforesaid circumstances as a whole would lead to a situation where it is not possible for the Court to digest the order of rejection of application filed by the petitioner. Accordingly, the impugned order deserves to be set aside." In the present case also from the averments made in the plaint, it cannot be clearly established that the plaintiff was aware of the fact of execution of the sale deed and during the life time of the plaintiff's father who has expired on 24.03.2013, and thereafter, the suit that has been filed is Page 21 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined beyond the period of limitation. 19. The judgment relied on 2019 SCC OnLine SC 372 in the case of Raghwendra Sharan Singh v. Ram Prasanna Singh (Dead) by Lrs. , it has held in paragraph Nos. 12, 13, 20, 22, 23, 24, 28, 29, 30 and 31 as under:- "12. It is further submitted by the learned counsel appearing on behalfof the appellant-original defendant that as held by this Court in catena of decisions while considering the application under Order 7 Rule 11 of the CPC , only the averments in the plaint are required to be considered. 13. It is further submitted by the learned counsel appearing on behalf of the appellant-original defendant that if clever drafting has created the illusion of a cause of action, as observed by this Court in a catena of decisions, the Court must nip it in the bud at the first hearing by examining the party searchingly under Order 10 of the CPC . It is further submitted that, therefore, as observed by this Court in the case of T. Arivandandam (supra), an activist judge is the answer to irresponsible law suits. It is submitted that, in the present case, if the bundle of facts narrated in the plaint and the averments in the plaint, as a whole, are considered, in that case, the suit is not only barred by law of limitation, but it is a vaxatious and meritless suit and, therefore, the plaint is required to be rejected in exercise of powers under Rule 7 Order 11 of the CPC . In support of his submissions, the learned counsel appearing on behalf of the appellant-original defendant has relied upon the decisions of this Court in T. Arivandandam (supra), Church of Christ Charitable Trust & Educational Charitable Society v. Ponniamman Educational Trust Page 22 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined (2012 8 SCC 706; A.B.C. Laminart Pvt Ltd. v. A.P. Agencies (1989) 2 SCC 163; Bloom Dekor Limited v. Subhash Himatlal Desai (1994) 6 SCC 322; Ssopan Sukhdeo Sable v. Assistant Charity Commissioner (2004) 3 SCC 137; Sham Lal alias Kuldip v. Sanjeev Kumar ( 2009) 5 SCC 548: AIR 2005 SC2897 and Ram Prakash Gupta v. Rajiv Kumar Gupta (2007) 10 SCC 59. Making the above submissions, it is prayed to allow the present appeal and quash and set aside the impugned order passed by the High Court as well as the trial Court rejecting Order 7 Rule 11 application and consequently to allow the said application and to reject the plaint in exercise of powers under Order 7 Rule 11 of the CPC . 20. Considering the averments in the plaint, it can be seen that, as such, the plaintiff has specifically admitted that the plaintiff and his brother executed the gift deed on 06.03.1981. It is admitted that the gift deed is a registered gift deed. It also emerges from the plaint that till 2003, neither the plaintiff nor his brother (during his lifetime) challenged the gift deed dated 06.03.1981 nor, at any point of time, claimed that the gift deed dated 06.03.1981 was a showy deed of gift. In fact, it is the defendant appellant herein who instituted the suit in the year 2001 against his brothers to which even the plaintiff was a party as defendant No. 10 and that was a partition suit filed by the appellant herein original defendant. It appears that the summon and the copy of the plaint - T.S. (Partition) Suit No. 203 of 2001 - was served upon the plaintiff in the year 2001 itself. Still, the plaintiff averred in the plaint that it came to the knowledge of the plaintiff with respect to the gift deed on 10.04.2003. Thus, it is born out from the averments in the plaint that, till 2003, the plaintiff never disputed the gift deed and/or never claimed that the gift deed dated 06.03.1981 was a showy deed of gift. With the aforesaid facts and circumstances, the application submitted by the appellantoriginal defendant to reject the plaint in exercise of powers under Order 7 Rule 11 of the CPC is required to be considered. 22. In the case of T. Arivandandam (supra), while Page 23 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined considering the very same provision i.e. Order 7 Rule 11 of the CPC and the decree of the trial Court in considering such application, this Court in para 5 has observed and held as under: "5. We have not the slightest hesitation in condemning the petitioner for the gross abuse of the process of the court repeatedly and unrepentently resorted to. From the statement of the facts found in the judgment of the High Court, it is perfectly plain that the suit now pending before the First Munsif's Court, Bangalore, is a flagrant misuse of the mercies of the law in receiving plaints. The learned Munsif must remember that if on a meaningful -- not formal -- reading of the plaint it is manifestly vexatious, and meritless, in the sense of not disclosing a clear right to sue, he should exercise his power under Order 7, Rule 11 CPC taking care to see that the ground mentioned therein is fulfilled. And, if clever drafting has created the illusion of a cause of action, nip it in the bud at the first hearing by examining the party searchingly under Order 10, CPC . An activist Judge is the answer to irresponsible law suits....." 23. In the case of Church of Christ Charitable Trust and Educational Charitable Society (supra), this Court in paras 13 has observed and held as under: "13. While scrutinizing the plaint averments, it is the bounden duty of the trial Court to ascertain the materials for cause of action. The cause of action is a bundle of facts which taken with the law applicable to them gives the Plaintiff the right to relief against the Defendant. Every fact which is necessary for the Plaintiff to prove to enable him to get a decree should be set out in clear terms. It is worthwhile to find out the meaning of the words "cause of action". A cause of action must include some act done by the Defendant since in the absence of such an act no cause of action can possibly accrue." 24. In A.B.C. Laminart Pvt. Ltd. v. A.P. Agencies, Salem (supra), this Court explained the meaning of Page 24 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined "cause of action" as follows: "12. A cause of action means every fact, which if traversed, it would be necessary for the plaintiff to prove in order to support his right to a judgment of the court. In other words, it is a bundle of facts which taken with the law applicable to them gives the plaintiff a right to relief against the defendant. It must include some act done by the defendant since in the absence of such an act no cause of action can possibly accrue. It is not limited to the actual infringement of the right sued on but includes all the material facts on which it is founded. It does not comprise evidence necessary to prove such facts, but every fact necessary for the plaintiff to prove to enable him to obtain a decree. Everything which if not proved would give the defendant a right to immediate judgment must be part of the cause of action. But it has no relation whatever to the defence which may be set up by the defendant nor does it depend upon the character of the relief prayed for by the plaintiff." 28. Applying the law laid down by this Court in the aforesaid decisions on exercise of powers under Order 7 Rule 11 of the CPC to the facts of the case in hand and the averments in the plaint, we are of the opinion that both the Courts below have materially erred in not rejecting the plaint in exercise of powers under Order 7 Rule 11 of the CPC . It is required to be noted that it is not in dispute that the gift deed was executed by the original plaintiff himself along with his brother. The deed of gift was a registered gift deed. The execution of the gift deed is not disputed by the plaintiff. It is the case of the plaintiff that the gift deed was a showy deed of gift and therefore the same is not binding on him. However, it is required to be noted that for approximately 22 years, neither the plaintiff nor his brother (who died on 15.12.2002) claimed at any point of time that the gift deed was showy deed of gift. One of the executants of the gift deed - brother of the plaintiff during his lifetime never claimed that the gift deed was a showy deed of gift. It was the appellant herein original defendant who filed Page 25 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined the suit in the year 2001 for partition and the said suit was filed against his brothers to which the plaintiff was joined as defendant No. 10. It appears that the summon of the suit filed by the defendant being T.S. (Partition) Suit No. 203 of 2001 was served upon the defendant No.10 plaintiff herein in the year 2001 itself. Despite the same, he instituted the present suit in the year 2003. Even from the averments in the plaint, it appears that during these 22 years i.e. the period from 1981 till 2001/2003, the suit property was mortgaged by the appellant hereinoriginal defendant and the mortgage deed was executed by the defendant. Therefore, considering the averments in the plaint and the bundle of facts stated in the plaint, we are of the opinion that by clever drafting the plaintiff has tried to bring the suit within the period of limitation which, otherwise, is barred by law of limitation. Therefore, considering the decisions of this Court in the case of T. Arivandandam (supra) and others, as stated above, and as the suit is clearly barred by law of limitation, the plaint is required to be rejected in exercise of powers under Order 7 Rule 11 of the CPC . 29.At this stage, it is required to be noted that, as such, the plaintiff has never prayed for any declaration to set aside the gift deed. We are of the opinion that such a prayer is not asked cleverly. If such a prayer would have been asked, in that case, the suit can be said to be clearly barred by limitation considering Article 59 of the Limitation Act and, therefore, only a declaration is sought to get out of the provisions of the Limitation Act , more particularly, Article 59 of the Limitation Act . The aforesaid aspect has also not been considered by the High Court as well as the learned trial Court. 30.Now, so far as the application on behalf of the original plaintiff and even the observations made by the learned trial Court as well as the High Court that the question with respect to the limitation is a mixed question of law and facts, which can be decided only Page 26 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined after the parties lead the evidence is concerned, as observed and held by this Court in the cases of Sham Lal alias Kuldip (supra); N.V. Srinivas Murthy (supra) as well as in the case of Ram Prakash Gupta (supra), considering the averments in the plaint if it is found that the suit is clearly barred by law of limitation, the same can be rejected in exercise of powers under Order 7 Rule 11(d) of the CPC . 31.In view of he above and for the reasons stated above, we are of the opinion that both the High Court as well as the learned trial Court have erred in not exercising the powers under Order 7 Rule 11 of the CPC and in not rejecting the plaint in exercise of powers under Order 7 Rule 11 of the CPC . For the reasons stated above, the impugned judgment and order passed by the High Court as well as the trial Court cannot be sustained and the same deserve to be quashed and set aside. Consequently, the impugned judgment and order passed by the High Court dated 12.03.2013 as well as the order passed by the Munsif, Danapur rejecting the Order 7 Rule 11 application filed by the original defendant are hereby set aside. Consequently, the application submitted by the appellant hereinoriginal defendant to reject the plaint under Order 7 Rule 11 of the CPC is hereby allowed and the plaint, being Title Suit No. 19 of 2003 is hereby rejected. The present appeal is allowed accordingly in terms of the above. No costs." 20. The said judgment shall also be applicable to the facts of the present case as from the plaint it can be clearly established that the plaintiffs were aware of the execution of sale deed during the life time of their father. Page 27 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined 21. The judgment that has been relied on by the learned advocate for the plaintiff reported in 2025 (0) AIJEL-HC 249968 in the case of Lh. Of Patel Naranbhai Karmasibhai V. Lh. Of Harijan Dhyabhai Bhudarbhai, it has held in para No.13 & 17 as under:- "13. It is pertinent to mention at this stage that plea of rejection of plaint is plea of demurer and it allows defendant to scuttle suit at threshold without examining merits. Needless to say that when Court is called to decide the rejection of the plaint under Order 7, Rule 11 of the CPC , only averments made in the plaint requires to be read and no defence can be seen pleaded either in written statement or application filed under Order 7 Rule 11 of the CPC . The Court has to accept that pleadings of the plaintiff is germane, so far as decision on Order 7 Rule 11 of CPC is concerned and if it is found that statement made in the plaint is barred by any provision of law, the Court can reject the plaint. However on a plain reading of the plaint along with the documents annexed therewith, I am of the considered opinion that no such finding can be rendered at this stage unless appropriate issue is framed and evidence is led in respect of the applicability of the relevant Articles as canvassed by the parties. Over and above that, it is required to be mentioned that in an application under Order 7, Rule 11(d) of CPC , this Court cannot adjudicate such an issue on the basis of bare averments in the plaint. 17. Considering the above provision, if the facts of the present case, as discussed hereinabove, are examined, in that event, it would be found out that as soon as the respondents herein came to know about the revenue record, they have submitted an application Page 28 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined before the revenue authority for the release of mortgaged-deed executed between the parties, however, the said application had not been entertained, however thereafter, they have preferred an application to the Public Information Officer, Sub Registrar, Tharad and sought certain information on 01.09.2016, however, the said application had not been entertained by the office of the Sub-Registrar, Tharad, therefore, the original plaintiffs have preferred an application to the revenue authority and on the basis of the said application, an inquiry was carried out by the office of the Additional Collector, Tharad and they have sought certain information from the office at the relevant point of time and the SubRegistrar, Tharad had forwarded the documents to the learned Deputy Collector, Tharad on 15.05.2017 along with the copy of index prepared by the office on the basis of mortgage-deed executed by and between the forefathers of the plaintiffs and the defendants and thus admittedly on 15.05.2017, they came to know about the actual position and thus, the period of three years would start to commence from the said date and admittedly in the year 2019, the suit has been filed for the reliefs as prayed for in the plaint, which in my considered opinion cannot be said to be filed beyond a period of three years. However, it can be said to be mix question of of facts and law and that can be decided at the time of deciding the suit. It is required to be noted that the averment made in the plaint appears to be candid and does not intend to camouflage the intention of the plaintiffs to circumvent Order 7 Rule 11 of the CPC . The averment made in the plaint on close introspection does not indicate a bogus or a frivolous litigation or an illusory drafting to obviate the obstacle of Order 7 Rule 11 of the CPC . The application filed under Order 7, Rule 11 of the CPC appears to have been done as a matter of course as the averments made in the same suggest." The fact remains that in the present case, the plaintiff has categorically stated in the plaint that the father of the Page 29 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined plaintiff has expired because of the act of the defendant. The fact that the plaintiff was also aware about the consent decree that has been drawn in Special Civil Suit No.57 of 2012, wherein the father of the plaintiff has given consent for executing sale deed in favour of defendants, therefore, the fact that there was fraud committed on the father of the plaintiff and the sale deed was executed is a got of story and by clever drafting the plaintiff has tried to file suit within the period of limitation. The rest of the judgments relied on by the plaintiff will not be applicable to the facts of the present case as in the present case, the plaintiff himself has admitted that the father of the plaintiff was aware during his life time about the execution of the sale deed and the fact of consent term having been filed in Special Civil Suit No.57 of 2012, also corroborated the fact that the father of the plaintiff had admitted their signature on the documents executed by the father of the plaintiff in the year 2005 and the fact that the plaintiff has come out with story that the plaintiff has come to the knowledge of fact of execution of sale deed only when Page 30 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined the notice under Section 135(d) was issued on the plaintiff and from the documents produced on record it cannot be clearly established that the last notice that was served by the Land Revenue Department was on 24.02.2023, and the suit having been filed on 11.05.2016 is beyond the period of limitation. The said document that has been produced with additional affidavit by the defendant though is not forming part of the plaint but the fact remains that the plaint, plaintiff has referred to the fact of notice being received under section 135(d) of the Land Revenue Code and it is settled law that where a document is sued upon and in terms are not set out in the plaint but referred to in the plaint, the said documents get incorporated by reference in the plaint, and therefore, the document with respect to last service of notice under section 135(d) of the Land Revenue Code will have to be referred to. Moreover, no particulars of fraud has been given in the plaint and the plaintiff has also stated of fraud being committed on the father of the plaintiff, the law in that matter is very clear under Order vi Rule 4 of Code of Page 31 of 32 Uploaded by MANOJ KR. RAI(HC01072) on Tue Jun 10 2025 Downloaded on : Wed Jun 11 00:58:55 IST 2025 NEUTRAL CITATION C/CRA/323/2017 JUDGMENT DATED: 10/06/2025 undefined Civil Procedure, which are as under:- "4. Particulars to be given where necessary. - In all cases in which the party pleading relies on any misrepresentation, fraud, breach of trust, willful default, or undue influence, and in all other cases in which particulars may be necessary beyond such as are exemplified in the forms aforesaid, particulars (with dates and items if necessary) shall be stated in the pleading." 22. Therefore, there has to be details of fraud as the fraud relies on misrepresentation, fraud breach of trust, undue reference and therefore, the particular are to be given. 23. For the foregoing reasons, the impugned order dated 13.04.2017 is not sustainable and the same deserves to be quashed and set aside and is hereby quashed and set aside. The Civil Revision Application is allowed. The plaint, abovementioned, fails and stands rejected. Rule is made absolute. No order as to costs.
